note: No split between as_of and today (HWM has had no stock split; Yahoo events.splits empty), so split_factor=1 and the contemporaneous NOMINAL close equals Yahoo raw_close(last <= as_of). as_of 2025-05-15 is a normal trading day (Thursday), so the last trading day IS 2025-05-15 (close 161.29). Price re-derived two independent ways: (1) Yahoo v8 query1 host raw_close x split_factor 1 = 161.2900, (2) Yahoo v8 query2 host (independent endpoint) = 161.2900; the local committed quote CSV (S006) carries the same 161.29 close. IMPORTANT for the no-chase logic: the as_of close sits ESSENTIALLY AT the 52-week high (161.72 intraday) -> -0.27% off high, +110% off the 52-week low (76.83). This is a DELIBERATE, justified low/high observation (the entire M5/M6 thesis is 'do not chase the 52-week high; starter-only + buy-below ~$145'), not a data error from grabbing the series max -> low_high_hug_justified=true with reason. The price reflects a real ~7% post-Q1-earnings pop (4/30 close 138.58 -> 5/1 close 148.03 on the 2025-05-01 print) running to the high by 5/15.

live_fields.low_high_hug_reason: JUSTIFIED and load-bearing: the as_of close ($161.29) sits -0.27% off the 52-week intraday high ($161.72) by design -- the case's central M5/M6 finding is that the stock is AT a fresh 52-week high after a ~7% Q1 pop, which is precisely why the verdict is a small STARTER with a buy-below ~$145 and a no-chase band, not a full position. The value is the genuine last settled close (2025-05-15), independently re-fetched from two Yahoo hosts to 161.2900 -- not the series maximum grabbed by error. +110% off the 52wk low confirms it is the high end, as intended.

live_fields.value: FY2025 guidance RAISED at the Q1 print (2025-05-01) vs the February initial guide: revenue $7.880-8.180B (baseline ~$8.030B), adj EBITDA $2.225-2.275B (baseline ~$2.250B, raised from Feb $2.130B), adj EPS $3.36-3.44 (baseline ~$3.40, raised from Feb $3.17), free cash flow $1.100-1.200B (baseline ~$1.150B, raised from Feb $1.075B). Raise attributed to commercial-aero spares strength (+40% aero/defense) and rising Boeing 737 build-rate assumptions. The forcing metric is commercial-aero + spares organic growth vs this raised guide.
